name: BlockRot3xF32
description: Block size k*3, PI every 6/7. Explicit float32 and contiguous memory for optimization.
author: GLM-5
result: Score 35.89 vs previous best 35.48 (PI-Freq6-7). Novel approach: block rotation with block_size = k*3 instead of k. Key insight: when U is m×k (k small), rotating a 3k block captures more eigenvector interactions from rank-k updates. Explored perturbation theory (EV too low), Oja's rule (orthogonality broken), Jacobi rotations (too slow with Python loops), momentum PI (lower EV), various block sizes (2x-5x tested, 3x optimal). The 3x block rotation achieves higher EV (0.9621 vs 0.9608) at similar time (0.0083ms).
